name: HWM_TM_COST_OVERRIDES
description: Cost overrides entered against the reported times for a person and a date

columns.Name: TM_COST_OVERRIDE_ID
columns.Comments: Primary Key column containing a random generated number.  This column is not updateable.

columns.Name: TM_COST_OVERRIDE_VERSION
columns.Comments: Part of Primary Key column with incremental value starting from 1 for every unique TM_COST_OVERRIDE_ID. It will be used as version controller of changes made.

columns.Name: COST_DATE
columns.Comments: Respective Date for which Cost Override details is being applied

columns.Name: START_TIME
columns.Comments: Either can hold Start time or Empty to represent Start of the day

columns.Name: STOP_TIME
columns.Comments: Either can hold End time or Empty to represent End of the day

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: LATEST_VERSION
columns.Comments: Y/N. Indicates if this is the most recent version of the cost override entry.

columns.Name: DELETE_FLAG
columns.Comments: Y/N. Indicates if the Cost override entry has been deleted or not.

columns.Name: DATE_FROM
columns.Comments: validity start time of the row

columns.Name: DATE_TO
columns.Comments: validity end time of the row

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
